questionHtml: Explain, using the equation in (a) and the graphs, why the presence of visible matter alone cannot account for the velocity of stars when r > R0.
markschemeHtml:
for v to stay constant for r greater than R0, M has to be proportional to r

 

but this contradicts the information from the M-r graph

OR

if M is constant for r greater than R0, then we would expect v ∝ r − 1 2

 

but this contradicts the information from the v-r graph

 

[2 marks]

meta.title: Explain, using the equation in (a) and the graphs, why the presence of visible matter alone cannot account for the velocity of stars when r > R0.
